note_data.title: Books of prime entry
keyPoints: The process of accounting starts once the book-keeper finishes recording the financial transactions
Accounting deals with measuring, identifying, and communicating financial information recorded by the book-keeper which facilitates the preparation of financial statements
Bookkeeping involves recording financial transactions from source documents of a business whereas accounting deals with maintaining financial statements from the bookkeeping records
Assets are the resources owned and controlled by a company, with the formula: Assets = Capital + Liabilities
Current Assets are assets which have a monetary value or can be converted into cash within 12 months, examples include account receivables and cash
Non-Current Assets, also known as Fixed Assets, are assets acquired to earn revenue for the company and used for more than 12 months without being intended for resale
Capital is the number of resources and funds provided by the owner injected into the business, with the basic accounting equation: Capital = Assets - Liabilities
Liability is the money owed to another firm or an entity, with the formula: Liabilities = Assets - Capital
Goodwill refers to the intangible asset that represents the reputation, customer loyalty, brand value, and other non-physical attributes of a business

definitions.definition: The resources owned and controlled by a company
An asset which has a monetary value or can be converted into cash within 12 months
Also known as Fixed Assets, these assets are acquired to earn revenue for the company and used for more than 12 months without being intended for resale
The number of resources and funds provided by the owner injected into the business
The money owed to another firm or an entity

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Name the specific book of prime entry without explanation
Give characteristics or features of the book of prime entry

modelAnswer.question: Explain why businesses use books of prime entry. [3 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: Books of prime entry provide a chronological record of transactions which makes it easier to trace transactions back to source documents. They reduce errors because transactions are recorded systematically from source documents before posting to ledger accounts. They save time because similar transactions are grouped together making the posting process more efficient.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Chronological record/easier to trace transactions

commonMistakes: Confusing bookkeeping with accounting - bookkeeping records transactions while accounting maintains financial statements
Incorrectly applying debit and credit rules - an increase in an asset is a debit and a decrease is a credit
Mixing up current and non-current assets based on the 12-month timeframe criterion
examinerTips: Remember the basic accounting equation: Capital = Assets - Liabilities
Understand that goodwill may be subject to impairment if its value declines over time
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Flow diagram showing source documents → books of prime entry → ledger accounts, with examples of each book (sales day book, purchases day book, cash book, journal) and their corresponding source documents
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (3 marks) — they asked students to identify which books of prime entry would be used when a business sells a non-current asset for cash, testing knowledge of both asset disposal recording and cash receipts
N/D 2022 P1 Q15 (1 mark) — focused on advantages of maintaining books of prime entry, with common wrong answers including confusion between books of prime entry and ledger accounts

M/J 2024 P2 Q6 (4 marks) — required students to state two advantages of using books of prime entry, with marks awarded for control account preparation, information summarization, and ledger detail reduction
N/D 2023 P1 Q12 (1 mark) — tested which business documents are NOT entered in books of prime entry, catching students who confused source documents with subsidiary books

workedExample.question: ABC Trading made the following transactions on 15 March 2024: Sold goods on credit to Khan Ltd $500, Received cash from Ahmed $300 for goods sold last month, Purchased stationery for cash $50. State which book of prime entry would be used to record each transaction. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Identify the nature of each transaction - credit sale, cash receipt, cash purchase
Step 2: For credit sale to Khan Ltd $500 - this involves goods sold on credit terms
Step 3: For cash received from Ahmed $300 - this is cash coming into the business
Step 4: For stationery purchased for cash $50 - this is cash going out of the business
Step 5: Match each transaction type to the appropriate book of prime entry
workedExample.answer: Credit sale to Khan Ltd $500 - Sales Day Book; Cash received from Ahmed $300 - Cash Receipts Book; Stationery purchased for cash $50 - Cash Payments Book
workedExample.markScheme: 2 marks per correct book of prime entry identified (Sales Day Book, Cash Receipts Book, Cash Payments Book). Deduct 1 mark for each incorrect identification. Accept 'Sales Journal', 'Cash Book Dr side', 'Cash Book Cr side' as alternatives.

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused the Cash Book with petty cash book when dealing with small cash transactions' — always distinguish between main cash transactions (Cash Book) and minor office expenses (Petty Cash Book)

Examiner Report 2024: 'Students frequently wrote ledger account names instead of books of prime entry' — remember that Sales Ledger is NOT a book of prime entry, the correct answer is Sales Day Book or Sales Journal

Examiner Report 2022: 'Candidates lost marks by not specifying whether cash transactions go in receipts or payments side' — always state 'Cash Receipts Book' or 'Cash Payments Book', not just 'Cash Book'
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw a flow diagram showing: Source Documents → Books of Prime Entry → Ledger Accounts → Trial Balance. Include arrows showing the flow from invoices/receipts to day books/cash book, then to individual ledger accounts. Label each book of prime entry (Sales Day Book, Purchases Day Book, Returns Inward Day Book, Returns Outward Day Book, Cash Book, Petty Cash Book) with examples of transactions recorded in each.
note_data.quickRecap: Accounting begins after bookkeeping records financial transactions and involves measuring, identifying, and communicating financial information to prepare financial statements. Assets are resources owned by a company and are categorized as current (convertible to cash within 12 months) or non-current (used for more than 12 months). The fundamental relationship between assets, capital, and liabilities is expressed through the basic accounting equation: Capital = Assets - Liabilities.
